evals.name: list-refactor
evals.prompt:
You are reviewing a C file for corner case programming. The `insert_at` function in /Users/marwan.burelle/code/ai-toolbox/skills/corner-cases/research/exercises/list/list-orig.c handles inserting an element into a sentinel-based linked list at a given position. The author split the logic into special cases (empty list, end of list, middle). Review this function for corner case programming and produce a refactored version.

Goals:
- Preserve observable behavior (same result for every input).
- Eliminate any TRUE corner cases (redundant guards that the general logic already handles).
- Minimize cyclomatic complexity and exit points.
- If you find any latent bugs exposed by removing guards, fix them.

Save the refactored file to OUTPUT_DIR/list.c (OUTPUT_DIR is provided to you as an absolute path). Also write OUTPUT_DIR/NOTES.md with a brief explanation of what you changed and why.
